# RMVMR

## Installation

RMVMR can be installed from the [MRCIEU
R-Universe](https://mrcieu.r-universe.dev/) with

``` r
install.packages("RMVMR", repos = c("https://mrcieu.r-universe.dev", "https://cloud.r-project.org"))
```

To install `RMVMR` directly from the GitHub repository, first make sure
you have the `remotes` package installed:

``` r
install.packages("remotes")
```

Then the `RMVMR` package can be installed using:

``` r
remotes::install_github("WSpiller/RMVMR")
```

To update the package just run the following command again.

``` r
remotes::install_github("WSpiller/RMVMR")
```

## Description

We have written the `RMVMR` R package to perform radial multivariable
Mendelian randomization analyses, including heterogeneity statistics for
assessing instrument strength and validity. The package accommodates any
number of exposures less than 6, and is currently includes a range of
functions for estimating causal effects, as well as assessing
conditional instrument strength and pleiotropic bias.

## Citation

Spiller, W. Bowden, J and Sanderson E. Estimating and visualising
multivariable Mendelian randomization analyses within a radial
framework. medRxiv, 2023.04.04.23288134, doi:
<https://doi.org/10.1101/2023.04.04.23288134>

## License

This project is licensed under GNU GPL v2.

# Package index

## All functions

- [`format_rmvmr()`](https://wspiller.github.io/RMVMR/reference/format_rmvmr.md)
  : format_rmvmr
- [`ivw_rmvmr()`](https://wspiller.github.io/RMVMR/reference/ivw_rmvmr.md)
  : ivw_rmvmr
- [`mrmvinput_to_rmvmr_format()`](https://wspiller.github.io/RMVMR/reference/mrmvinput_to_rmvmr_format.md)
  : Convert an object of class MRMVInput from the MendelianRandomization
  package to the RMVMR rmvmr_format class
- [`pleiotropy_rmvmr()`](https://wspiller.github.io/RMVMR/reference/pleiotropy_rmvmr.md)
  : pleiotropy_rmvmr
- [`plot_rmvmr()`](https://wspiller.github.io/RMVMR/reference/plot_rmvmr.md)
  : plot_rmvmr
- [`rawdat_rmvmr`](https://wspiller.github.io/RMVMR/reference/rawdat_rmvmr.md)
  : Raw multivariable MR summary data using lipid fractions as exposures
  and systolic blood pressure as an outcome.
- [`strength_rmvmr()`](https://wspiller.github.io/RMVMR/reference/strength_rmvmr.md)
  : strength_rmvmr
